title: E2E Test Infrastructure: harness, helpers, and fixture management
summary: Provide reusable test infrastructure for E2E tests: process spawning harness, GitHub API helpers, environment/config management, cleanup, and fixture isolation. Foundation for all E2E test scenarios (I2–I7).

stakeholder_intent.goal: Execute E2E tests against real golemic_e2e sandbox with automatic setup, cleanup, and artifact isolation
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Reliable, reproducible E2E testing without manual GitHub operations; CI-friendly
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: Tests can spawn golemic processes, create/delete issues, verify PRs/reviews, cleanup without affecting other tests

in_scope: Process harness: exec.Command to run golemic binary
Environment setup: load golemic_e2e config, set tokens, manage working directory
GitHub API helpers: createTestIssue, deleteTestIssue, assertPRExists, assertReviewExists
Test fixture management: broken configs, invalid tokens, stale branches/PRs
Cleanup: defer-based automatic teardown per test
Logging: capture stdout/stderr from golemic subprocess
Isolation: each test uses unique issue number to prevent collisions
out_of_scope: CI/CD pipeline setup (only test code structure)

preconditions: golemic binary can be built: go build ./cmd/golemic
golemic_e2e repo exists and is cloned locally
Test tokens (GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN, GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN) available as env vars or in ~/.golemic/golemic_e2e/credentials.json
gh CLI installed and authenticated for test runs

business_rules.rule: Each test must clean up all artifacts (issues, PRs, branches, worktrees)
Each test uses unique issue number to prevent collisions
business_rules.outcome: Parallel tests on different issue numbers are safe

business_rules.rule: Tokens must never appear in test logs or output
business_rules.outcome: Redact tokens from logs before assertion

business_rules.rule: Test must fail fast if golemic_e2e config is invalid
business_rules.outcome: Preflight errors caught before subprocess spawn

errors.message: config.json parse error or missing fields

process_steps.name: Load test dependencies
process_steps.action: Import harness, helpers, load golemic_e2e config, verify tokens

process_steps.name: Run golemic subprocess
process_steps.action: Spawn golemic run --issue N with harness; capture output

process_steps.name: Cleanup
process_steps.action: Delete PR, branch, issue; verify worktrees cleaned

integration_contracts.idempotency: Issue numbers unique per test; deletion idempotent
integration_contracts.retry_policy: Manual retry via test re-run; limited retries (3 attempts) for transient GitHub API errors
integration_contracts.compatibility: gh CLI >= 2.0; GitHub REST API v3

integration_contracts.retry_policy: No automatic retry; test controls logic via exec.Command timeout and signal handling
integration_contracts.compatibility: Go 1.21+; POSIX-compliant shell; binary must be available

side_effects.description: Test logs captured and optionally printed on failure

security.authentication: GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN and GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN; tokens must not appear in test output
authorization_rules: Tokens scoped to write golemic_e2e repo
Tests run with user's GitHub auth only; no service account needed
audit_requirements: Test names and pass/fail results logged

architecture_constraints: Harness must not modify golemic source code
Environment setup must use golemic_e2e's existing .golemic/config.json
All cleanup must be idempotent (safe to call twice)
allowed_changes: Add test Go files and helpers
forbidden_changes: Do not modify cmd/golemic or internal/runner
Do not modify golemic_e2e production files
Do not hardcode GitHub tokens (use env vars)
implementation_context.migration_strategy: Test infrastructure is additive; no breaking changes to existing code
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Each test creates unique issue number (e.g., appends timestamp); parallel test execution safe as long as different issues used

acceptance_scenarios.title: Harness loads golemic_e2e config and tokens
Subprocess spawned and output captured
then: Output does not contain tokens (redacted if needed)

acceptance_scenarios.title: Helpers assert PR and review existence
Cleanup removes all test artifacts
given: Test created issue, PR, branch, worktree

quality_commands: go test ./test/e2e -v (all tests with verbose output)
non_functional_requirements: Cleanup must complete within 10s (timeout on stale resources)
Logs must not exceed 100MB per test run
Parallel tests safe: up to 10 concurrent safely (depends on GitHub API limits)
definition_of_done: test/e2e/ directory created with harness.go, github.go, fixtures.go, cleanup.go
Cleanup verifiable (can run test twice; second run still clean)
README in test/e2e/ documents usage and extending tests

decision_log.decision: Place test code in golemic/test/e2e/, not in golemic_e2e. Test harness and helpers are part of golemic tooling, not the sample project.
decision_log.rationale: Clean separation: tool code vs. test fixtures. Allows CI integration without exposing test-specific code to sample project.

decision_log.decision: Use defer-based cleanup in each test; idempotent operations safe to call multiple times. No centralized cleanup script.
decision_log.rationale: Rust/Go pattern; each test is self-contained and safe to run in isolation.

decision_log.decision: Use exec.Command (Go stdlib) to spawn golemic binary as subprocess; capture stdout/stderr.
decision_log.rationale: No external dependencies; matches existing test patterns in golemic (cmd/golemic/main_test.go already uses this).

decision_log.decision: Each test creates issues with unique identifiers (timestamp + test name appended); prevents collisions in parallel runs.
decision_log.rationale: GitHub issue numbers auto-increment; tests can safely create sequential issues without conflicts.

codebase_evidence.finding: Existing test pattern: subprocess spawning and output capture via exec.Command
Runner struct manages orchestration; tests will instantiate and call via subprocess
Manual smoke test shows expected environment setup: .golemic/config.json, tokens, gh auth
